title: Add topologySpreadConstraints and PriorityClass support
summary: Adds optional spec.topologySpreadConstraints and spec.priorityClassName fields to KeystoneSpec, wires them into buildKeystoneDeployment with a default zone-spread TSC, adds webhook validation for PriorityClass existence and TSC label selector correctness, regenerates CRD manifests, and updates HA documentation. The implementation is clean and well-structured but has two blocking gaps: only one default TSC (zone) instead of the required two (zone + hostname), and missing RBAC permissions for the webhook's PriorityClass lookup.

tests_checklist.item: Default TSC test exists and passes
Custom TSC override test exists and passes
Empty TSC disables defaults test exists
PriorityClassName set test exists and passes
PriorityClassName nil test exists and passes

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (helper functions, Gomega tests)
No dead code or unused imports
Feature ID (CC-0075) referenced in all comments

security_checklist.item: Webhook validates PriorityClass existence at admission
Webhook validates TSC label selector correctness
RBAC permissions cover all API calls

architecture_checklist.item: Client injection follows existing webhook pattern
Helper functions follow existing containerResources/probes pattern
CRD manifests in sync between config/crd and helm/crds

fail_fast_checklist.item: Invalid PriorityClass rejected at admission time
Invalid TSC label selectors rejected at admission time
Non-API errors (Get failures) reported as InternalError

defensive_checklist.item: Nil Client guard prevents panic in webhook
Nil PriorityClassName dereferenced safely via guard
Nil LabelSelector handled before accessing MatchLabels

security_findings.description: Missing RBAC permissions for scheduling.k8s.io/priorityclasses. The webhook's validate() calls w.Client.Get(ctx, ..., &schedulingv1.PriorityClass{}) at keystone_webhook.go:378, but no +kubebuilder:rbac marker exists for scheduling.k8s.io/priorityclasses, and the Helm ClusterRole template (_helpers.tpl) has no scheduling.k8s.io rule. At runtime, the webhook will receive 403 Forbidden errors when validating PriorityClass existence.
security_findings.fix: Add '// +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=scheduling.k8s.io,resources=priorityclasses,verbs=get;list;watch' to keystone_controller.go (alongside existing markers). Add a scheduling.k8s.io/priorityclasses rule with get;list;watch verbs to the rbacRules template in operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl. Regenerate CRD/RBAC manifests.

issues_found.description: Only 1 default TopologySpreadConstraint (zone) is generated instead of 2 (zone + hostname). The acceptance criteria state: 'sets default TopologySpreadConstraints of maxSkew: 1 over topology.kubernetes.io/zone and kubernetes.io/hostname, both whenUnsatisfiable: ScheduleAnyway'. The implementation only returns a single zone-level constraint.
issues_found.fix: Add a second TopologySpreadConstraint with TopologyKey 'kubernetes.io/hostname', MaxSkew 1, WhenUnsatisfiable ScheduleAnyway, and the same LabelSelector. Update the default TSC test to assert HaveLen(2) and verify both constraints.

issues_found.description: Missing RBAC for webhook PriorityClass lookup. The webhook calls w.Client.Get for schedulingv1.PriorityClass but no kubebuilder RBAC marker or Helm ClusterRole rule grants get on scheduling.k8s.io/priorityclasses. The operator's service account will receive 403 Forbidden at runtime.
issues_found.fix: Add '// +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=scheduling.k8s.io,resources=priorityclasses,verbs=get;list;watch' marker to keystone_controller.go. Add scheduling.k8s.io/priorityclasses rule to the Helm rbacRules named template in _helpers.tpl.
issues_found.evidence: No match for 'scheduling|priorityclass' in kubebuilder markers or Helm ClusterRole templates. Webhook code: w.Client.Get(ctx, types.NamespacedName{Name: *k.Spec.PriorityClassName}, pc)

issues_found.description: Missing test: TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_EmptyTopologySpreadConstraintsDisablesDefaults. Task 1.2 item 3 requires a test that sets spec.TopologySpreadConstraints to an empty non-nil slice and verifies no TSCs are present. This test is absent. Additionally, the current code uses 'len(...) > 0' which returns defaults for an empty slice, so the behavior doesn't match the design intent of 'set means full replace'.
issues_found.fix: Add the missing test. If empty-slice-disables-defaults is desired, change the guard from 'len(...) > 0' to a nil check ('keystone.Spec.TopologySpreadConstraints != nil'). If empty-slice-gets-defaults is intended, update the task description and add a test confirming that behavior.

issues_found.description: HA documentation LabelSelector column is incorrect. The docs table states the default TSC uses labels 'app.kubernetes.io/name: keystone, app.kubernetes.io/component: api' but the actual selectorLabels() function returns 'app.kubernetes.io/name: keystone, app.kubernetes.io/instance: <name>'. The component label is wrong; it should be the instance label.
issues_found.fix: Change 'app.kubernetes.io/component: api' to 'app.kubernetes.io/instance: <name>' in the documentation table to match selectorLabels() at reconcile_deployment.go:146-151.

issues_found.description: HA documentation is missing a recommended PriorityClass YAML definition. Acceptance criteria state: 'a recommended PriorityClass definition operators should ship alongside Keystone for production'. The docs only provide prose guidance, not a concrete YAML manifest.
issues_found.fix: Add a recommended PriorityClass YAML example (e.g., value: 1000000, globalDefault: false, description: ...) to the HA documentation.
issues_found.evidence: Acceptance criteria: 'including a recommended PriorityClass definition operators should ship alongside Keystone for production'. Docs provide only prose.

issues_found.description: HA documentation is missing the updated component table with a Keystone row. Acceptance criteria state: 'Update the Component table to add a Keystone row with replicas=3, ScheduleAnyway topology spread + PDB, and PriorityClass support.'
issues_found.fix: Add a Keystone row to the existing HA component table with: replicas=3, topology spread (ScheduleAnyway, zone+hostname), PDB (minAvailable: 1), PriorityClass (optional).
issues_found.evidence: Acceptance criteria: 'Update the Component table to add a Keystone row'. The git diff shows only a new section was added; no existing table was modified.

suggested_improvements: Replace custom labelsEqual() function at keystone_webhook.go:430 with maps.Equal() from the Go standard library (available since Go 1.21, project uses Go 1.25). This eliminates a trivially reimplemented stdlib function.

Consider adding a TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_DefaultTopologySpreadConstraints_LabelSelectorMatchesSelectorLabels test that explicitly verifies the default TSC LabelSelector equals selectorLabels(ks) — the current test does this but making it a named assertion strengthens intent.
next_steps: Add the missing hostname TSC default to topologySpreadConstraints() and update the default TSC test to assert 2 constraints
Add +kubebuilder:rbac marker for scheduling.k8s.io/priorityclasses and add the rule to Helm rbacRules template in _helpers.tpl
Add TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_EmptyTopologySpreadConstraintsDisablesDefaults test and decide on nil-vs-len guard semantics
Fix HA docs: correct LabelSelector labels, add PriorityClass YAML example, add Keystone row to component table
